spec_match.rationale: The page now matches the ChromeStatus framing: a Windows-only TCP port randomization change with no JavaScript API surface, demonstrated through collision and retry-pressure simulation.

interactive.rationale: The concept has strategy, connection-rate, TIME_WAIT, and port-range controls wired to a live canvas and stats model rather than a static code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and diagnostic copy now explain both the birthday-collision rollout constraint and the side-channel motivation ChromeStatus describes.

edge_cases.rationale: The simulator surfaces boundary range saturation, sequential predictability, random retry pressure, and RFC 6056-style scatter behavior.

references.rationale: The page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/5106900286570496 self-heal link.

design_system.rationale: CSS uses shared design variables, button background and color are paired, and canvas colors are read from the design-system variables.

openQuestions.title: Run the required chrome-devtools-mcp browser verification
openQuestions.detail: Chrome DevTools MCP is not exposed in this Codex session, so this source-review refresh cannot honestly claim the required browser pass. Before fully closing this critique, open /v139/randomizing-tcp-port-allocation-on-windows/birthday-collision-simulator/ with chrome-devtools-mcp, exercise the strategy select, both sliders, port-range select, and Simulate button, then record console, network, DOM/readout, and conformance-route evidence.

summary: Source review repaired a broken inline script, corrected the stale ChromeStatus feature id, fixed the simulator's port-range and peak-in-flight calculations, and added visible edge-case diagnostics. ChromeStatus API was checked for feature 5106900286570496; official browser verification remains open because chrome-devtools-mcp is unavailable in this environment.
